title: CENTRAL OREGON PARTNERSHIPS FOR YOUTH - 211info
text: DESCHUTES COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE CENTRAL OREGON PARTNERSHIPS FOR YOUTH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a one-on-one volunteer mentorship program aimed at reducing trauma for children impacted by parental incarceration. Mentors commit to spending a few hours each week for at least one year with a child. Activities may include attending community events, participating in sports or recreational activities (such as biking, playing at the park, art projects, swimming, bowling, hiking), or visiting the library or movies. Some mentors provide academic support, help build reading skills, and connect children and families to additional community resources, including scholarship opportunities and youth enrichment programs. 63333 W Highway 20 Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Ages 5-15 with an incarcerated parent or caregiver Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in, call, or email for more information. Referrals are also accepted from service providers such as Family Access Network (FAN) or mental health providers. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Deschutes County Services: Mentoring Programs Other Services Offered At This Location: CRIMINAL CORRECTIONAL SYSTEM - JAIL INFORMATION LAW ENFORCEMENT AGENCIES - SHERIFF'S OFFICE SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - DEFLECTION DRUG TREATMENT PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. DESCHUTES COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE
